title: crisp fried xtra crunchy veracruzan shrimp tacos
description: a vibrant, crunchy, and flavorful twist on traditional mexican shrimp tacos, featuring a hint of brandy and a crispy exterior.

ingredients.name: 2 pounds large shrimp, peeled and deveined

instructions: in a shallow dish, mix together the almond flour, paprika, cayenne pepper, and a pinch of salt.
dip each shrimp into the flour mixture, coating both sides evenly.
heat 1/4 inch of coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
add the coated shrimp and sear for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown and cooked through.
transfer the shrimp to a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
in the same skillet, add the remaining 1/4 inch of coconut oil over medium heat.
add the garlic and sauté for 1 minute, until fragrant.
add the lime juice, cilantro, and mint, and stir to combine.
warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
to assemble the tacos, place a few shrimp on each tortilla, followed by a spoonful of the garlic-sauce mixture.
optional: top with diced radishes, sliced avocado, or a dollop of sour cream.
